preamble: The reduction of 'e' to 'ə' (schwa) is the ultimate fate of many unstressed vowels, moving toward a neutral, central position.

examples.note: Widespread reduction in the history of English.

sources: Lass, R. (1984). Phonology: An Introduction to Basic Concepts. Cambridge University Press.
Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2015). A Course in Phonetics (7th ed.). Cengage Learning.
